message:
chore(beads): close bd-2nyj (conformance report + coverage summary)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -323,7 +323,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-2ni","title":"E2E: Rust extension runtime smoke suite (sample) with verbose logs","description":"Background:
- We need an end-to-end validation that sampled extensions run in Rust with real wiring.

Steps:
- Build an E2E script that loads each sampled extension from `docs/extension-sample.json` and runs its scenarios.
- Select runtime tier **generically** based on artifact/manifest (WASM host vs PiJS JS tier); no per-extension branching.
- Capture stdout/stderr, tool outputs, and event hook logs per extension.
- Store logs as artifacts for debugging (per-extension log file).

Logging requirements:
- Structured logs with correlation IDs: extension id, scenario id, tool/command/event type.

Acceptance:
- E2E script passes for the full sample and produces logs suitable for triage.

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
- [ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
- [ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
- [ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
- [ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
- [ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes
","acceptance_criteria":"[ ] Scope in description implemented fully with no feature loss
[ ] Unit tests cover core success/failure + edge cases for this bead
[ ] Integration/E2E script exercises the end-to-end path (or explicitly marks N/A with rationale in notes) and emits detailed JSONL logs + artifacts per bd-4u9
[ ] Logs include inputs, outputs, timing, and redaction summary; artifacts list is deterministic
[ ] Quality gates pass: cargo fmt --check, cargo check --all-targets, cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings, cargo test
[ ] Docs/fixtures updated if behavior or UX changes","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-03T02:46:59.333523573Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T22:22:11.849394111Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T22:22:11.849263919Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-2i5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-34f","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-3qo","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-3rr","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-3so","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-2ni","depends_on_id":"bd-ic9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T00:00:01Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2nng","title":"Enable syntax highlighting in rich_rust for code blocks","description":"Enable syntax highlighting for code blocks in tool output and assistant responses.

## Current State
- rich_rust has 'syntax' feature available but not enabled
- Code blocks from tools (read, bash, grep) show without highlighting
- glamour has optional syntax-highlighting feature also unused

## Implementation
1. Enable 'syntax' feature in Cargo.toml for rich_rust
2. Create helper in PiConsole to detect language and apply highlighting
3. Use Syntax renderable for code output in tool results
4. Integrate with existing truncation logic

## Binary Size Impact
- Adds ~2MB (syntect with embedded syntax definitions)
- Consider: Could use glamour's syntax-highlighting instead (shared dep)

## Language Detection
- File extension mapping (rs->rust, py->python, etc.)
- Shebang detection for scripts
- Fallback to 'text' for unknown

## Test Plan
- Unit test: syntax highlighting produces colored ANSI for known languages
- Manual test: read tool output for .rs file shows Rust syntax colors

## Files to Modify
- Cargo.toml (add syntax feature)
- src/tui.rs (add syntax highlighting method)
- src/tools.rs (use highlighting in read/grep output)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:46.125262433Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-04T22:16:35.126638605Z","closed_at":"2026-02-04T22:16:35.126576650Z","close_reason":"PiConsole markdown: syntax-highlight fenced code blocks via rich_rust::Syntax + unit test","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2nng","depends_on_id":"bd-20lm","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:08:46.135805889Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"bd-2oal","title":"Acquire + snapshot unmodified extensions","description":"# Goal
Vendor the selected extensions **without modification** into a reproducible artifact set with integrity metadata.

# Scope
- Download exact upstream release artifacts (npm tarballs, GitHub releases, templates).
- Store under tests/ext_conformance/artifacts with consistent layout.
- Record source, version, license, and checksums for each artifact.

# Non‑Goals
No compatibility testing here; this bead only snapshots upstream bits.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:19:56.532022060Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-07T04:30:45.105114627Z","closed_at":"2026-02-07T04:30:45.105016253Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2oal","depends_on_id":"bd-3o8d","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:21:22.369423941Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"bd-2oal","depends_on_id":"bd-ofcj","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:19:56.564714946Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":320,"issue_id":"bd-2oal","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Background: To claim unmodified compatibility, we must vendor exact upstream artifacts with traceable provenance.

Reasoning: Snapshotting enables deterministic tests and avoids drift when upstream releases change.

Considerations: Record checksums, licenses, and sources; avoid any local edits to artifacts.","created_at":"2026-02-05T07:45:42Z"},{"id":672,"issue_id":"bd-2oal","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"COMPLETED: All children closed. 208 artifacts vendored across npm/ (66), community/ (58), third-party/ (23), official (60), templates (1). Snapshot protocol defined (bd-1pqf), provenance verified 100% (bd-bc2z), risk reviewed (bd-3h8w), inclusion list finalized (bd-3vb8), metadata manifests created (bd-3uvd).","created_at":"2026-02-07T04:30:44Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-2ok9","title":"E2E: Message injection + session control with verbose logging","description":"End-to-end test for message injection and session control APIs.

## Scenarios

### 1. Message Injection Flow
- Extension sends pi.sendMessage({customType: 'bookmark'})
- Verify message appears in session JSONL
- Verify display=true shows in TUI output

### 2. User Message Injection
- Extension sends pi.sendUserMessage('test prompt')
- Verify agent turn triggered
- Verify steering modes work (steer interrupts, followUp queues)

### 3. Session Metadata Control
- Set session name via pi.setSessionName()
- Verify name persists in session file
- Set entry label via pi.setLabel()

### 4. Tool Management
- Disable a tool via pi.setActiveTools()
- Verify tool no longer available
- Re-enable and verify restored

### 5. Model Control
- Change model via pi.setModel()
- Verify provider switch
- Adjust thinking level and verify

## JSONL Logging Format
```json
{"ts": "...", "component": "extension_api", "api": "sendMessage", "params": {...}, "result": {...}, "duration_ms": N}
```

## Required Log Events
- message_inject_start
- message_inject_complete
- turn_trigger{source: "extension", delivery: "steer"|"followUp"|"nextTurn"}
- session_metadata_update{field, old_value, new_value}
- tool_state_change{tool, enabled}
- model_change{from, to}

## Artifact Output
- logs/e2e_message_session_{timestamp}.jsonl
- Session JSONL diff before/after

## Files
- tests/e2e/message_session_control.rs","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:20:44.344569665Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:06.616931274Z","closed_at":"2026-02-05T05:43:06.616867044Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-2ok9","depends_on_id":"bd-1rqs","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-04T21:20:50.586273514Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}
